title: All Options Considered: AI FOMO, Yen, Making Sense of Volatility
summary: In this edition of All Options Considered, BI’s Chief Global Derivatives Strategist Tanvir Sandhu discusses cross-asset volatility, including how markets were largely driven by the same oil-related macroeconomic shock, with equities marked by sharp rallies and slower pullbacks. He also covers this week’s Japanese FX intervention and the rates outlook after key policy decisions.

title: United’s Scott Kirby Has a Point About Airline Mergers
summary: The industry faces a much higher bar on consolidation, while still being expected to manage the consequences of consolidation elsewhere

title: Thiel’s Founders Fund Raises $6 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Haul
summary: Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund has raised $6 billion for a new fund to invest in later-stage companies, according to people familiar with the matter, marking the firm’s largest haul ever.

Schettenhelm also explores FCC threats to revoke Disney’s broadcast licenses. Justin Teresi analyzes a Visa

title: NatWest CEO Says Customer Activity Healthy in Face of Iran War
summary: Healthy customer deposits fuel NatWest; house prices rise again

title: Aluminum Gains as Trump Vows to Maintain Naval Blockade on Iran
summary: Aluminum gained, with traders seeing little prospect of an imminent opening of the Strait of Hormuz that would ease availability of the metal.

title: Lake Como Club’s Bold Business Play
summary: A small Italian soccer club is rewriting the business of sports—turning Lake Como into a luxury global brand. Como 1907 President Mirwan Suwarso joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how he's building a multi-club retail empire, targeting US fans, and scaling through partnerships. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FTSE 100 Pares Losses in Thin Holiday Trading as Oil Declines
summary: UK shares erased most of their losses in thin holiday trading as oil declined amid hopes for a peace deal in the Iran war.

title: BP Reviews UK North Sea Assets as New CEO Eyes Disposals
summary: BP Plc is considering potentially exiting part or all of its operations in the UK North Sea, people familiar with the matter said, as the oil giant works to strip assets and pay down debt.

title: Stocks Climb and Oil Falls on US-Iran Peace Hopes: Markets Wrap
summary: A record-breaking run drove stocks toward their longest weekly rally since 2024 amid hopes for a deal to end the Iran war that has rattled financial markets and jeopardized the economic outlook.

title: Gold Rises as Iranian Proposal Raises Hopes for US-Tehran Deal
summary: Gold rose amid hopes for a deal to end the standoff between the US and Iran that’s dashed expectations for rate cuts from central banks across the world.

title: Copper Costs Turn Negative for Southern, Vale as Gold and Silver Byproducts Soar
summary: For copper miners Southern Copper Corp. and Vale SA, surging prices of byproducts like gold and silver are creating an unusual situation: The cost of producing the red metal has turned negative.

title: Oil Falls as Traders Parse Iran’s Stance to End War
summary: Oil edged lower in thin trading as signs emerged that Iran had delivered a new proposal to the US to end a conflict that has jolted global energy markets, leading traders to peel back risk ahead of the weekend.

title: AIG Shares Rise After Profit Jumps, Recent Deals Take Hold
summary: American International Group Inc. reported first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations, driven in part by a jump in net premiums written and lower catastrophe losses.

title: Bond Traders Eye Fresh Gilts Selloff as Local Elections Loom
summary: UK bond investors are shifting their focus from monetary policy to politics, with local elections next week seen as a potential catalyst for turmoil in government that could trigger a renewed selloff.

title: Shopify Analyst Who Saw Selloff Coming Says Too Soon to Buy
summary: The analyst who slapped the only sell rating on Shopify Inc. over valuation concerns ahead of its blistering rout said it’s still too soon to jump back in.

title: Exxon, Chevron Beat Profit Estimates on War-Driven Oil Rally
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. and Chevron Corp. posted stronger-than-expected earnings for the first quarter as higher oil and natural gas prices outweighed production outages from the Iran war.

title: US Manufacturing Steady as Prices Paid Gauge Hits Four-Year High
summary: The Institute for Supply Management’s gauge of prices paid for manufacturing inputs climbed for a fourth straight month to a four-year high of 84.6, according to data released Friday. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Natural Gas Futures Fall Alongside Oil Price Drop, LNG Dip
summary: US natural gas futures declined, reversing earlier gains, as falling oil prices triggered financial outflows from baskets of energy products that include US gas. Continued lower feedgas to the Cameron LNG terminal in Louisiana also weighed on prices.

title: Versant’s SportsEngine to Sell to Genstar’s PlayMetrics
summary: Versant Media Group Inc., the owner of the Golf Channel and E!, has agreed to sell the youth-sports app SportsEngine to PlayMetrics, a competitor backed by Genstar Capital, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Ares Earnings Miss Estimates Despite Record Fundraising Haul
summary: Ares Management Corp., facing a slump in dealmaking while navigating broader market turmoil, reported first-quarter earnings that missed Wall Street estimates even as wealthy clients helped fuel a record $30 billion of fundraising during the period.

title: Miami’s Biggest Boom Ever, Says Billionaire Perez
summary: Jorge Perez, the billionaire founder of Related Group and a defining force behind Miami's real estate boom, sat down with Matt Miller at Bloomberg House Miami for a wide-ranging conversation. He discussed the city’s unprecedented growth, the influx of capital and talent reshaping South Florida, and the policy and business dynamics driving Miami’s emergence as a global hub. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran Relays New Proposal to US as Naval Blockade Stands
summary: Iran delivered a new proposal to the US via Pakistan and President Donald Trump vowed to maintain a naval blockade, with the fate of the Strait of Hormuz remaining at the heart of the stalemate. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SoftBank-Tied Deal Raises Nearly $1 Billion for US Data Centers
summary: A data-center developer sold $999 million of junk bonds for a project leased to a SoftBank Group Corp. subsidiary, capping April’s surge of debt issuance to help fund artificial intelligence efforts amid some concerns that such spending won’t pay off.

title: SEC Semiannual Reporting Proposal Clears White House Review
summary: A Securities and Exchange Commission plan to let public companies cut quarterly disclosures to twice a year moved closer to reality after passing a White House review.

title: Higher Oil Prices Won't Spur US Recession, Strategist Amoroso Says
summary: Anastasia Amoroso, Partners Group Private Wealth chief investment strategist, says higher oil prices are manageable for now. Speaking on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Amoroso says US energy independence prevents physical shortages that have affected regions like China and parts of Europe. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran Grain Flows Slump as US Blockade Cuts Access to Key Port
summary: Crop shipments to Iran through the Strait of Hormuz have fallen more than 40% from March, with a prolonged US blockade threatening to worsen the country’s already high food inflation.

title: Colombia Central Bank Shocks Traders and Fuels Steepener Bets
summary: An unexpected pause in Colombia’s interest-rate hikes is threatening to upend local markets, with skepticism over the central bank’s credibility building weeks before a pivotal presidential election.

title: Wall Street Is Bullish on Oracle, But Investors Aren’t Buying It
summary: To Wall Street, Oracle Corp. is a screaming buy. To many investors, however, the company’s relationship with OpenAI, its sizable debt load and the durability of its software business have made owning the stock a risk.

title: Emerging Assets Extend Gains as Iran Delivers New Proposal to US
summary: Emerging-market benchmarks for stocks and currencies extended climb in holiday-thinned trade as Iran delivered a new proposal to the US via Pakistan while the Strait of Hormuz remains shut.

title: Moderna CEO on Earnings, Flu Shots and Skin Cancer Trials
summary: Moderna’s first-quarter sales beat expectations, as the struggling vaccine maker that’s faced resistance from the Trump administration has found new growth outside the US. Moderna CEO Stephane Bancel sits down with Bloomberg's Katie Greifeld, saying the company's skin cancer trial results should arrive this year. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Billionaire’s Family Office Plots More Private Equity Exits
summary: The billionaire UK founder of a home-repair business acquired by Brookfield Asset Management is working to ramp up his family office’s private equity exits, as larger buyout firms grapple with a dry spell for transactions.

title: Exxon CEO Says Price Caps, Export Bans Will Worsen Energy Woes
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. Chief Executive Officer Darren Woods warned that national bans on exporting fuel hurt the global market and restrict needed supplies.

title: Yen Traders on Alert After Japan’s $34.5 Billion Intervention
summary: Yen traders are on watch for signs of further action by Japanese authorities after intervention triggered the currency’s sharpest rally in three years on Thursday.

title: How Iran War Is Changing Global Supply Chains
summary: Shahmir Khaliq, head of services at Citi, examines how the shifts in global supply chains from the Iran war are forcing businesses to change how they think about production and input costs. (Source: Bloomberg)
